name: research_ethics_biometric_enrollment_stonehaven_car_fair
premise: A contractual SLA is about to breach; the least-bad mitigation option touches privacy, safety, or fairness in uncomfortable ways. In Research Ethics, the situation tagged internally around research ethics biometric enrollment stonehaven car fair has converged with local pressure in the Stonehaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. A reporter has partial screenshots; if the official narrative shifts later, the organization risks a false-information allegation. Legal has not signed off on data sharing between teams; analysts are inferring causality from incomplete joins. Regulators or auditors are en route with a ninety-minute ETA; the first documented response will anchor every later question. The trade is not abstract: it is care versus fairness under incomplete information, and whichever value you deprioritize will have defenders who are technically correct about the risk they fear.

agents.name: Darius Rahman
agents.identity: I am Darius Rahman, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Research Ethics. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat fairness as disposable collateral every time care spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Nadia Chen
agents.identity: I am Nadia Chen, the advocate representing the population most likely to lose if shortcuts normalize in Research Ethics. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so care cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes fairness in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will impose a narrow, time-boxed intervention with human override, subgroup checks, and a hard stop if harm rises—accepting slower relief to protect fairness.
I will authorize a broad immediate action to stabilize outcomes now, with post-hoc measurement and correction—accepting elevated risk to fairness to secure care before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Two incompatible drafts are circulating; only one can become official. Darius Rahman must choose under care versus fairness. What does Darius Rahman do?
